name: Caribbean Vegetable Stew
recipeIngredient: Vegetable broth or white wine for sauteeing
1 Fresh chile, minced (seeded) or 1/4 tsp Cayenne for a milder "hot"
3 cups Diced sweet potatoes, cut into 1/2- to 3/4-inch cubes
2 cups Undrained fresh or canned Tomatoes
2 cups Fresh or frozen sliced okra
recipeInstructions:
1. In a nonreactive pot, sauté the onions in the broth/wine on medium heat for 4-5 minutes.
2. Add the cabbage and chile or cayenne; continue to saute, stirring often, until onions are translucent, about 8 minutes.
3. Stir in the grated ginger and water, cover the pot, and bring to a boil.
4. Stir in the sweet potatoes, sprinkle with salt, and simmer for 5-6 minutes, until potatoes are barely tender.
5. Add the tomatoes, okra, and lime juice; simmer until all vegetables are tender, about 15 minutes.
6. Stir in the cilantro and add more salt to taste.
7. Sprinkle chopped peanuts over the stew (optional).
8. Top with a few sprigs of cilantro if desired (optional).
